articles.description: A new study found that chronic wasting disease can sometimes spread silently, with infectious prions present even in animals that show no symptoms. While there is no confirmed human risk, researchers say the disease’s ability to evolve and spread across species warrants close attention.

articles.title: Scientists found a way to explain bird flocks that “defy” Newton’s third law
articles.description: Physicists have solved a long-standing problem involving systems that appear to violate Newton’s third law, such as bird flocks and bacterial swarms. By adding carefully designed “imaginary partners” to their models, they can now simulate these complex systems with unprecedented accuracy.

articles.title: 'Dancing girl's' bare torso restored in Indian textbook after backlash
articles.description: A picture in a new school textbook had covered up the naked torso of the famous figurine with dark shading.

articles.title: Scientists just found a hidden weakness in forever chemicals
articles.description: Researchers discovered that hydrogen radicals generated by intense UV light can break down stubborn PFAS “forever chemicals” without added chemicals. The breakthrough reveals a key mechanism that could lead to greener and more effective technologies for permanently destroying these pollutants.

articles.title: Alien messages may have reached Earth without us realizing it
articles.description: A new SETI study suggests we may be overlooking alien signals not because they aren't there, but because their own stars are scrambling them before they escape into space. Turbulent plasma and powerful stellar storms can spread an ultra-narrow radio transmission across a wider range of frequencies,

articles.title: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
articles.description: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

articles.description: What a waste 45 million ballots and 60,000 people hired to count them by hand? What a waste of public money! articles.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
articles.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

articles.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
articles.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

articles.title: Ottawa aims to cut grocery bills with $3.2B food security strategy
articles.description: The multi-year plan, titled seeks to reduce the country’s dependence on international food imports and break the grip of a highly concentrated retail market.

articles.title: Letters, June 11, 2026: ‘Lighting up the sky’
articles.description: Anyone else? Was I the only one in the southwest to see about a 5-7 second bright white flash, travelling from west to east on Sunday at 11:55 p.m.? articles.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
articles.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

articles.title: Honey bees have their own personal flight paths and fly them with stunning precision
articles.description: Researchers tracked honey bees in the wild using a drone-based system and found that each bee follows its own highly consistent flight path. Some repeated their routes so precisely that they flew only centimeters from where they had flown before. Landmarks like trees helped keep them on track, while

articles.title: A dying star could create a new universe instead of a black hole
articles.description: What if some black holes aren’t black holes at all? A new theoretical study suggests that when a massive star collapses, it might not form a singularity hidden behind an event horizon. Instead, the collapse could trigger the birth of a tiny new universe inside the dying star. Driven by dark energy,
